board.title: Computer Security
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: Computer security builds on foundational disciplines such as cryptography and covers domains like access control, network security, and application security. This puzzle explores fundamental concepts spanning different parts of the field.

clues.question: Converts data into a fixed-length digest through a one-way function; cannot be converted back
Verifies a user's identity using credentials like passwords; HTTPS uses certificates from authorities like Let's Encrypt to verify sites
Credential passed to a service to prove identity or access rights; commonly used by web APIs
Monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic
Malicious software that harms or exploits computer systems; includes viruses, worms, and trojans
Distributed attack that floods a service with traffic, making it unavailable to users
Converts plaintext into ciphertext; can be converted back to plaintext
Determines what a user can access based on permissions; root users have unrestricted access in Linux
Verifies a user's identity using multiple authentication methods
Isolated environment, such as a virtual machine, for running untrusted programs
Verifies a message's authenticity and integrity; confirms the sender's identity
Attack that inserts SQL into a query to access unauthorized data; prevented through parameterized queries
Protocol providing secure communications over a network; successor to SSL
Routes network traffic through an encrypted tunnel across a public network
